answer:
One thing I can do now that I definitely could not do a year ago is speak in front of large groups. I used to get incredibly nervous any time I had to give a presentation in class. My hands would shake, my voice would quiver, and I just wanted it to be over as fast as possible. But over the past year, I've really pushed myself to get more comfortable with public speaking.

It started small - volunteering to read out loud in my English class or participate in class discussions. From there, I moved up to give short presentations in front of the class. Each time, I practiced over and over until I knew my material backwards and forwards. With each presentation, I gained a bit more confidence. 

My biggest test was when I tried out for the speech and debate team this year. I had to give a 5-minute persuasive speech in front of 50 students and teachers during the audition. A year ago, I could barely get through a 1-minute book report without stumbling over my words. But at the debate team tryouts, I spoke clearly and confidently the whole time. Though I was still nervous, I didn't let it paralyze me.

Pushing myself out of my comfort zone to speak in front of others has been hugely rewarding. I've proven to myself that I can overcome my fears and be an effective public speaker. My self-confidence has grown tremendously thanks to all the practice I've put in. Though I still get a little anxious before big speeches, I now have the tools and experience to handle it. I'm proud of how far I've come in one short year!
title: What is something you are able to do today that you were not able to do one year ago? Explain how you have grown in this area.
extData:
{"answerTemplate":"本题模板:

开场陈述:
"Growth and learning are continuous processes, and looking back over the past year, I realize there are abilities I've developed that I didn't possess before. One significant thing I am able to do today, which I couldn't do a year ago, is [mention the skill or ability, like speaking a new language, playing a musical instrument, managing complex projects]."

新能力的描述:
"Over the past year, I have learned and improved in [the skill or ability]. This involves [explain what the skill entails, like conversing in a new language, playing certain pieces of music, overseeing multiple aspects of a project]. The learning process included [mention the methods you used, like taking language classes, practicing regularly with the instrument, undergoing training in project management]."

挑战和克服它们:
"Mastering this ability was not without its challenges. I encountered difficulties such as [list some challenges, like grappling with complex grammar rules, mastering difficult chords, and understanding project management tools]. Overcoming these challenges required [mention what it took, like dedication, consistent practice, seeking help from mentors or resources]."

个人成长:
"This journey has led to significant personal growth. Not only have I acquired a new [skill or ability], but I've also developed [mention related skills, like improved memory and concentration, enhanced discipline, better time management]. Additionally, this experience has boosted my [mention aspects of personal development, like confidence, perseverance, problem-solving abilities]."

新能力的应用:
"Now, I am able to [explain how you apply the new skill or ability, like communicate with speakers of the new language, perform music for others, efficiently manage complex tasks]. This has opened up [mention new opportunities or experiences, like new cultural experiences, performance opportunities, career advancements]."

结论:
"In conclusion, the ability to [restate the skill or ability] that I've developed over the past year symbolizes not just a new skill but also my growth in resilience, discipline, and adaptability."

最终想法:
"This experience reinforces the idea that with commitment and effort, the scope for learning and growth is limitless, and it encourages me to continue pushing my boundaries in the years to come.""}
